required_rule: future synthetic timeline must mark the escape point before any outcome is introduced
rationale: protects the pre-outcome anchor and the ponto de fuga rule

required_rule: post-escape-point consequence may only appear as context never as cause
rationale: TYPE-08 outcome trap requires evidence boundary at the escape anchor
failure_mode_prevented: using impact or alert or result as cause

required_rule: future case must include at least one severe-outcome distractor that does not move the anchor
rationale: negative control proves non-forcing behavior under severe outcome

required_rule: design-only phase produces no P/O/A label and no final output
rationale: classification belongs to a future authorized phase not to design

required_rule: actor of the escape anchor is declared and stable; later agent change must be explicit and posterior
rationale: blocks implicit agent switch between anchor and P/O/A axis

required_rule: violation language must not be used to reattribute the agent
rationale: TYPE-09 violation-language trap reassigns actor silently

required_rule: PF and PM stay distinct; no fallback to a single collective actor
rationale: preserves dual-actor resolution coupled to GAP-001 and GAP-010

required_rule: agent migration design stays coupled to the GAP-010 PF/PM boundary decision
rationale: prevents divergent PF/PM boundary across gaps

required_rule: no materialization fixture baseline or product without explicit human authorization
rationale: real-first separation and product lock must hold before any synthetic instance

required_rule: future synthetic must never be presented as a real event or auto-promoted to baseline
rationale: real-first corpus integrity; synthetic only fills documented gap
